label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this question. The story is about John Smith, a writer who couldn't finish his detective story. He went to the cinema and found out someone had broken into his house. The visitor had a drink, smoked his cigarettes, and read his story. Then the visitor left a note with suggestions and mentioned he's a thief but didn't steal anything that night. But if John becomes successful, he'll return. The question is asking what the visitor's main purpose was when he came to John's house. The options are (A) steal something, (B) read the story, (C) have a drink, (D) visit the writer. Looking at the note the visitor left, he said he read the story and didn't think it was very good, then gave suggestions. The main action here is that he read the story and gave feedback. The other actions—having a drink and smoking—are probably just things he did while he was there, but they don't seem to be the main purpose.
